text.title: Claude Advanced Features Core Rules
text.summary: 7 testable rules covering Extended Thinking, Computer Use, Prompt Caching and Batch API. Each rule has an imperative statement, a rationale anchored to the Anthropic API contract, and a source citation.

rule.statement: For any Extended Thinking call, set `max_tokens >= budget_tokens + 4096` so the answer block cannot be truncated by the thinking budget.
rule.rationale: Thinking tokens count against `max_tokens`. If `max_tokens == budget_tokens` the answer block is silently cut mid-sentence with `stop_reason="max_tokens"`.

rule.statement: Never set `temperature` together with `thinking.type="enabled"`; never stream Extended Thinking unless the consumer parses `thinking_delta` events distinctly from `text_delta`.
rule.rationale: Extended Thinking has its own sampling semantics. The API rejects the combination with `temperature`; the SSE delta type differs from regular text, so a text-only stream parser produces garbled output.

rule.statement: Place the most stable content first in `system`, mark it `cache_control:{"type":"ephemeral"}`, and put all dynamic content (timestamps, user ids, request ids) strictly after the cached block.
rule.rationale: Prompt cache is a byte-identical prefix match. Any drift before the cached block invalidates the cache and you pay full input cost plus a 25% write surcharge.

rule.statement: Log `usage.cache_read_input_tokens` on every cached call and assert hit ratio > 0.70 over any rolling 100-call window.
rule.rationale: Cache misses are silent — the API returns 200 OK with full-priced tokens. Below ~70% hit ratio the write surcharge erodes the 90%-read savings.

rule.statement: For Batch API submissions use `custom_id` equal to your database primary key or job id, group requests by identical `model` and `max_tokens`, and poll `client.messages.batches.retrieve` at intervals ≥60s until `processing_status == "ended"`.
rule.rationale: Results return out of order, so `custom_id` is the only join key. Tight polling wastes quota and adds zero speedup since batches take minutes to hours.

rule.statement: Iterate Batch API results and treat any item with `result.type == "errored"` as a re-submit candidate; never assume success from a 200 on the batch endpoint.
rule.rationale: Failed sub-requests are returned in the results stream with `type="errored"`. They are not retried automatically; the caller must detect and resubmit.

rule.statement: Run Computer Use only inside a sandboxed VM with no host credentials, bound by per-action timeout and a max-iteration count, and route any unrecognised UI state to human review instead of auto-retry.
rule.rationale: Computer Use issues OS-level click/type/key actions. Without sandboxing and bounded iteration the agent can corrupt host state on an unfamiliar UI; auto-retry on hallucinated actions amplifies the damage.
